Rufflet

#0626 Bouffalant #0628 Braviary
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Rufflet is a Normal/Flying type Pokémon introduced in Generation 5.

Rufflet

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0627
Type Normal Flying
Species Eaglet Pokémon
Height 0.5 m (1′08″)
Weight 10.5 kg (23.1 lbs)
Abilities 1. Keen Eye
2. Sheer Force
Hustle (hidden ability)
Local № 0133 (Black/White)
0109 (Black 2/White 2)
0075 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0089 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0281 (Sword/Shield)
0221 (Legends: Arceus)
0365 (Scarlet/Violet)
0179 (The Isle of Armor)
0032 (The Indigo Disk)

Training

EV yield 1 Attack
Catch rate 190 (24.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 70
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Flying
Gender 100% male, 0% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Base stats

HP 70
250 344
Attack 83
153 291
Defense 50
94 218
Sp. Atk 37
71 190
Sp. Def 50
94 218
Speed 60
112 240
Total 350 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

#0628

Rufflet changes

  • In Generations 5-7, Rufflet has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Black They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.
White They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.
Black 2
White 2		 It stands up to massive opponents, not out of courage, but out of recklessness. But that is how it gets stronger.
X
Omega Ruby		 They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.
Sun They pick fights indiscriminately. They grow stronger and more powerful each time they faint or are injured.
Moon With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.
Ultra Sun With its powerful legs and sturdy claws, it can crack even the hard shells of Shellder and pluck out their insides.
Ultra Moon Known as a natural-born warrior, soon after its hatching, it will challenge its parent to a fight in order to gain their acceptance.
Sword If it spies a strong Pokémon, Rufflet can’t resist challenging it to a battle. But if Rufflet loses, it starts bawling.
Shield A combative Pokémon, it’s ready to pick a fight with anyone. It has talons that can crush hard berries.
Legends: Arceus Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.
Scarlet They pick fights indiscriminately. They grow stronger and more powerful each time they faint or are injured.
Violet With its sharp claws, this Pokémon pierces its prey, and then it pecks at them. Although it also consumes berries, it’s a carnivore at heart.

Moves learned by Rufflet

  • In other generations
  • 5
  • 6
  • 7
  • 8
  • 9
Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Rufflet does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Rufflet cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Egg moves

Rufflet learns the following moves via breeding or picnics in Pokémon Scarlet & Violet. Details and compatible parents can be found on the Rufflet gen 9 learnset page.

Type
Cat.
Power
Acc.
Rock SmashFighting 40 100
RoostFlying — —

Other languages

English Rufflet
Japanese ワシボン (Washibon)
German Geronimatz
French Furaiglon
Italian Rufflet
Spanish Rufflet
Korean 수리둥보 (suridungbo)
Chinese (Simplified) 毛头小鹰
Chinese (Traditional) 毛頭小鷹

 

English Eaglet Pokémon
Japanese ヒナわしポケモン
German Adlerküken-Pokémon
French Pokémon Aiglon
Italian Pokémon Aquilotto
Spanish Pokémon Aguilucho
Korean 새끼독수리포켓몬
Chinese (Simplified) 雏鹰宝可梦
Chinese (Traditional) 雛鷹寶可夢

Name origin

ruffle
to shake feathers
-let
suffix meaning ‘small’
#0626 Bouffalant #0628 Braviary
